behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is loader orchestration between manifest validation and module import. Concrete Unit miss: `validateManifest()` can fail correctly, but loader may still import plugin entry first (triggering side effects) before rejecting. Real-Infra check: local filesystem + in-process module loading is sufficient; no subprocess/network needed. LLM/Multi-step not applicable. Rejection gate for higher level: behavior text is about manifest/loader sequencing only; nothing requires OS/subprocess/external API.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin dir with an invalid `manifest.json` and an entry module that writes a sentinel file or mutates global state on import. Run loader end-to-end in-process and assert: plugin is rejected, rejection reason cites missing field, and sentinel side effect never occurs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test uses multi-module wiring and filesystem interactions, which matches Integration and would not be valid as pure Unit.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundary between dependency resolver and hook executor. Concrete Unit miss: topological sort function can return `[B,A]`, but executor may iterate unsorted plugin registry and call `A.onLoad()` first. Real-Infra: temp filesystem plugins plus in-process loader are enough; no subprocess/network. No LLM dependency or multi-agent chain. Rejection gate for higher level: behavior text only requires correct local orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Build two test plugins on disk: A depends on B; both `onLoad` push their name into a shared log. Run loader and assert log order is exactly `B` then `A`.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Assertion targets resolver-to-executor handoff across modules, which requires Integration.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Need combined behavior of cycle detection plus load suppression. Concrete Unit miss: cycle detector can flag `A<->B`, but loader may still import one plugin before aborting, causing partial initialization. Real-Infra: local plugin fixtures and in-process execution are sufficient. No OS/subprocess/network requirement; no AI dependency. Rejection gate for higher level: behavior text disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow because it is local graph + loader control flow.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependencies and `onLoad` side effects. Run loader and assert: cycle error is surfaced with both plugin names, neither entry module/onLoad side effect occurs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test verifies cross-module detection-to-execution contract, fitting Integration.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: This is sequencing across module importer and lifecycle invoker. Concrete Unit miss: a unit test of hook dispatcher could pass while actual loader calls `onLoad` before awaited dynamic import resolves/executes. Real-Infra: in-process Node runtime with real local module files catches this; subprocess/real network are not required. No LLM dependency. Rejection gate for higher level: behavior text is local import/hook order, not OS-level or external-system behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use a plugin entry module that sets a flag during top-level execution and exports `onLoad` that asserts the flag is already set; if not, it throws. Run loader and assert successful load proves import-execute happened before `onLoad`.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real wiring of importer and lifecycle invocation, which is beyond Unit but below System.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is error propagation + dependency-aware continuation across loader modules. Concrete Unit miss: isolated error-handler test may pass, while integrated loader aborts whole initialization after one thrown hook. Real-Infra: local plugin graph and in-process execution are enough; no subprocess/network. No AI/agentic path. Rejection gate for higher level: behavior text does not require external infrastructure or multiple agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: `Fail` throws in `onLoad`; `Independent` has no dependency on `Fail`; `Dependent` depends on `Fail`. Run loader and assert: `Independent.onLoad` runs, `Dependent.onLoad` does not, and failure for `Fail` is reported without global abort.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Covers multi-module state handoff (dependency graph + error handling), matching Integration.
